line_level_findings.finding: Detector is a TWO-LAYER design, not allowlist-only: (1) `_MIXED_CASE_TECH_ALLOWLIST` (21 entries) protects explicitly-known ambiguous tech terms first; (2) `_is_camel_concatenation_artifact` then applies a heuristic — a token is judged an artifact ONLY when its CamelCase split (`_CAMEL_HUMP_RE`) yields 2+ segments and EVERY segment is >=3 chars AND is itself a member of `_ARTIFACT_SPLIT_WORDS` (= pre-existing `_KEYWORD_BOILERPLATE` union'd with a curated JD-structure/role-label word list). This is conservative by construction: it only fires when ALL segments are recognizably generic JD words, so real product/tech names (which essentially never split into all-boilerplate words) are not killed even when absent from the allowlist.
line_level_findings.evidence: apps/api/app/routers/cover_letters.py lines ~141-207 on the branch, read directly

line_level_findings.finding: Adversarial false-positive probe (reviewer-authored, not from the branch's own tests): ran `_is_camel_concatenation_artifact` against 42 real-world mixed-case tech/brand terms NOT in the branch's own test list or allowlist (iPhone, DevOps, GraphQL, OpenAI, PyTorch, TensorFlow, ServiceNow, PowerBI, LogRocket, ReactJS, VueJS, NextJS, Kubernetes, Terraform, GoLang, ObjectiveC, SwiftUI, AngularJS, RabbitMQ, ZooKeeper, HashiCorp, etc.). Result: 0/42 false positives.
line_level_findings.evidence: Reviewer script executed in the branch worktree this session (0/42 flagged) [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE]

line_level_findings.finding: Adversarial false-negative probe: ran the same function against 28 synthetic JD-glue-style tokens NOT in the branch's own artifact test list (SalaryRange, CompanyOverview, TeamCulture, ApplyNow, WorkLocation, OfficeLocation, BackgroundCheck, ReportsTo, PermanentPosition, GraduateProgram, etc.). Result: 9/28 NOT caught (e.g. SalaryRange, WorkLocation, BackgroundCheck, ReportsTo, GraduateProgram) because their second/first segment ('range', 'work', 'check', 'reports→to', 'graduate→program') is not itself in the curated `_ARTIFACT_SPLIT_WORDS` set.
line_level_findings.evidence: Reviewer script executed this session, 9/28 false negatives (of made-up probe terms, not the live-verified reported artifacts) [VERIFIED-WITH-FRESH-EVIDENCE]
line_level_findings.severity_assessment: NOT FAIL-level. The finding (MV-cover-letter-studio-006 / NF-final-PII-001, severity MEDIUM) is scoped to the specific live-observed artifacts ('ManagerLocation', 'EngineerLocation', 'ResponsibilitiesBuild', 'EmployerShare') and the general 'CamelCase gets rewarded' root cause — all 4 are caught (confirmed: 'location' and 'responsibilities' are already in the pre-existing `_KEYWORD_BOILERPLATE` set the new set unions with). The design deliberately trades recall (catching every conceivable glue artifact) for precision (never killing a real skill/tech term) — the correct trade-off for a user-facing 'skills you match' panel, where a false positive (hiding a real skill) is worse than an occasional missed boilerplate artifact (cosmetic). This is the OPPOSITE of the FAIL-level 'allowlist-only, kills unlisted legit terms' pattern the brief warned against — measured false-positive rate is 0/42 on terms outside its own test suite.

line_level_findings.finding: Regression check: ran the pre-existing cover-letter-related suites (test_mv_cluster_a_cover_letter.py, test_mv_resume_grounding.py, test_mv_clstudio_j_residuals.py) against the branch. 66 passed / 7 failed; all 7 failures are exactly among the 18 pre-existing MissingResumeError failures documented in EXIT-G06-FINAL-serialized.md (unrelated to CamelCase scoring, being fixed separately by fix/g06-noresume-test-regressions) — no NEW regression introduced by this branch.

branches.required_change_before_resubmit: Add an `except MissingResumeError as exc:` clause to the PIPELINE branch of run_agent_job (apps/api/app/workers/tasks.py, inside the `if job['agentKey'] == 'pipeline':` block, ahead of the existing `except Exception as exc:` at line 174), mirroring the same honest-completion + refund pattern already used for the single-agent branch and already used for the pipeline's own NoChangesApplied handling inside `_pipeline_core` (apps/api/app/routers/agents.py:1452-1457) — but scoped to the WORKER's job-terminal-state write (mark_completed + refund_pipeline_outstanding), not the _pipeline_core return value, since a mid-pipeline MissingResumeError currently propagates as a raised exception rather than a returned no-op the way NoChangesApplied does. Add a new test seeding a `pipeline`-agentKey BackgroundJob whose underlying step raises MissingResumeError and asserting the job completes honestly (status='completed', no 'missingresumeerror' in the message, quota refunded) — mirroring `test_pipeline_partial_refund_on_midrun_crash`'s monkeypatch-of-`_run_pipeline_body` pattern. Do not weaken or remove the existing single-agent fix or its test.

checks.item: (5) refund_pipeline_outstanding is the correct refund semantic for pipeline jobs
checks.result: PASS — read apps/api/app/repositories/background_jobs.py: `refund_single_reservation` (line 246) is scoped to the SINGLE fixed enqueue-time reservation of a single-agent job (boolean `quotaReserved`/`quotaRefundedAt` flag scheme) — wrong for a pipeline job, which can reserve MULTIPLE metered runs across its steps (tailor + coverLetter, tracked via `quotaReservedCount`/`quotaRefundedCount` integer counters incremented per-step under `_pipeline_job_ctx`, see `increment_reserved`/`increment_refunded` lines 282-305). `refund_pipeline_outstanding` (line 307) correctly computes and refunds THIS job's own `quotaReservedCount - quotaRefundedCount` outstanding balance, scoped and idempotent. The new MissingResumeError branch in the pipeline try/except calls `repo.refund_pipeline_outstanding(job_id)` — the IDENTICAL call the pipeline's own pre-existing crash path (`except Exception as exc:`, unchanged) already uses one clause below it. Parity with the established in-branch pattern confirmed by direct source comparison.

re_review.summary: All 5 requested checks independently verified with fresh evidence. The fixer's stacked commit 3409976 correctly implements the exact required_change_before_resubmit from the original round: a `except MissingResumeError as exc:` clause added to the PIPELINE branch of run_agent_job, positioned ahead of the generic except Exception, using the same honest str(exc)-only message and the pipeline-correct refund_pipeline_outstanding call, plus a new test mirroring the existing crash-refund test's seam. The reviewer's own original adversarial probe — which had FAILED against bf8cbc3 — now passes against 3409976. No scope creep, no prohibited patterns, no swallowing of other exception classes, correct refund semantic. VERDICT: PASS.

overall_summary: fix/g06-noresume-test-regressions (f68a4e6): PASS (round 1). fix/cover006-camelcase (0f4c4b8): PASS (round 1). fix/nf-pii-002-async-refuse: FAIL at bf8cbc3 (round 1, incomplete — pipeline async code path unpatched, verified live via reviewer adversarial probe) -> PASS at 3409976 (re-review, stacked fixer commit closed the exact gap identified in round 1; independently re-verified with fresh evidence, including re-running the SAME adversarial probe that originally failed). All three branches are now PASS as of their latest SHA (f68a4e6, 0f4c4b8, 3409976 respectively).
